##NAMES=Molybdomenite
##RRUFFID=R230009
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=PbSe^4+^O_3_
##LOCALITY=El Dragón mine, Porco Municipality, Antonio Quijarro Province, Potosí, Bolivia
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Jingnan (Tom) Zhang
##DESCRIPTION=This is the polytype molybdomenite-P2_1_/c. Pale yellow prismatic individual crystals on a matrix of krut'aite, also associated with dolomite, chalcomenite, molybdomenite.  Known as α-PbSe^4+^O_3_. Data from study by Zhang et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R230009
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Pb_1.00_Se_1.00_O_3_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 9.154 b: 8.071 c: 8.78 alpha: 90 beta: 103.15 gamma: 90 volume: 631.6 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
